key_event.1789-1798: French Revolution: mass mobilization amid collapsing central authority
key_event.1799-1808: Napoleon's despotic Leviathan: centralization, codification, plebiscitary empire
key_event.1809-1818: Napoleon's fall and the Charter of 1814
key_event.1819-1828: Liberal public sphere grows despite Ultra reaction and censorship
key_event.1829-1838: 1830 July Revolution absorbed by elite July Monarchy
key_event.1849-1858: Louis-Napoléon's 1851 coup and the authoritarian Second Empire
key_event.1859-1868: Liberal Empire concessions: strike legalization, press/assembly liberalization
key_event.1869-1878: Third Republic founded; Seize Mai defeat entrenches parliamentary supremacy
key_event.1879-1888: Republican consolidation: Ferry Laws, press/union freedoms
key_event.1889-1898: Dreyfus Affair mobilizes intellectuals; Ligue des droits de l'homme founded
key_event.1899-1908: Church-state separation and Dreyfus resolution consolidate secular republic
key_event.1909-1918: WWI mobilization and Union sacrée expand the wartime state
key_event.1919-1928: Franc crisis and stabilization under Poincaré (1926)
key_event.1929-1938: Popular Front mobilization amid weak, unstable Depression-era state
key_event.1939-1948: Defeat, Vichy, Liberation, and postwar dirigiste/welfare state-building
key_event.1949-1958: Fourth Republic instability, colonial defeats, then 1958 Gaullist restoration
key_event.1959-1968: Fifth Republic consolidation; May 1968 mass mobilization
key_event.1969-1978: Giscardian liberalizing reforms and post-1968 civic mobilization
key_event.1979-1988: Decentralization and rights reforms offset by nationalization-privatization wash
key_event.1989-1998: Maastricht integration and liberalization erode autonomous national state
key_event.1999-2008: CPE protests and EU treaty rejection: society checks state
key_event.2009-2018: State of emergency and security-law expansion after terror attacks
key_event.2019-2028: Red Queen standoff: Yellow Vests, pension strikes, 49.3
